steps.title: Factor the left side
steps.content: Rewrite $-5x + 20px = 90$ by factoring x: $$(-5 + 20p)x = 90$$ A linear equation of the form $ax = b$ has no solution when $a = 0$ and $b \neq 0$ (the equation becomes $0 = 90$, a contradiction). So we need $-5 + 20p = 0$.

steps.title: Solve for p and test all choices
steps.content:
$$20p = 5 \implies p = \frac{5}{20} = \frac{1}{4}$$
  • Choice A — $0$: $p=0$ gives $-5x = 90$, which has solution $x = -18$. One solution, not no solution. ✗
  • Choice B — $\frac{1}{4}$: $-5 + 20 \cdot \frac{1}{4} = -5 + 5 = 0$, so $0x = 90$ — no solution. ✓
  • Choice C — $\frac{3}{2}$: $-5 + 20 \cdot \frac{3}{2} = -5 + 30 = 25 \neq 0$, so one solution exists. ✗
  • Choice D — $\frac{9}{2}$: $-5 + 20 \cdot \frac{9}{2} = -5 + 90 = 85 \neq 0$, so one solution exists. ✗
That matches Choice B — $\frac{1}{4}$.
